disclaimer: Phase 2 in progress. Volume fields are populated only where a per-licence registry record has been located; otherwise they remain null and the row stays at placeholder or needs_verification per docs/NRTA_PHASE_2_PLAN.md §4. Nothing in this file is legal advice, regulatory evidence, or a final claim about any project, operator, First Nation, or watershed. See docs/NRTA_PHASE_1_README.md and docs/NRTA_PHASE_2_PLAN.md.

sourceRecords.supports: Per-licence allocated m³/yr, source watershed, conditions for Water Act licences in Alberta.
limitations: Some licence conditions are redacted in the public viewer
Coverage of historic NRTA-era licences is uneven

sourceRecords.supports: Process and cooling-water volumes per industrial facility (separate from the Water Act licence).
limitations: Approvals are published as PDFs, not a structured registry

sourceRecords.registryName: Impact Assessment Agency of Canada — Project Registry
sourceRecords.supports: Project-by-project environmental impact statements including water-use sections.
limitations: Not every NRTA-province project is federally assessed
Water sections vary in granularity

sourceRecords.supports: Federal-jurisdiction pipeline and LNG water permits and conditions, including hydrostatic test water and watercourse crossings.
limitations: Provincial-jurisdiction operations are out of scope

sourceRecords.registryName: Alberta Electric System Operator (AESO) — Connection Queue
sourceRecords.supports: Phase I/II queue status and aggregate MW load for AB data-centre cluster projects.
limitations: Queue is a capacity-planning artifact, not a water-licensing record
Position does not imply approval

sourceRecords.registryName: Indian Oil and Gas Canada (IOGC) — Water Licence Registry
sourceRecords.supports: Per-Reserve federally-administered water rights preserved under NRTA s.1.
limitations: Per plan §3, full ledger access likely requires an ATIP request
Coverage of contemporary use is unclear from the public site

sourceRecords.registryName: IAAC — Genesee Generating Station Expansion Project (reference 80059)
sourceRecords.supports: Confirms Capital Power Generation Service Inc. is the proponent for the Genesee site; the 2014 federal decision found no environmental assessment required for the natural-gas expansion (Genesee 4 & 5, ~1,050 MW combined, ~17 km north of Warburg, AB).
limitations: Decision predates the Polaris @ Genesee Energy Campus data-centre proposal (publicly announced 2026)
Page contains no water-use volumes, no source water body, and no Water Act licence numbers
Establishes site proponency only; not a substitute for the AB Authorizations Viewer record

sourceRecords.registryName: Saskatchewan Water Security Agency — Water Allocation
sourceRecords.supports: Issues Water Rights Licences for non-domestic water use in Saskatchewan; the licence specifies annual allocation, source, and conditions. Required for all non-domestic use, and for domestic use exceeding 5 cubic decametres.
limitations: Public ledger of per-licence records is less directly queryable than the Alberta Authorizations Viewer; per-licence drill-down may require contacting the agency or filing a request
Phase 2 plan §3 flags this registry as 'less publicly indexed' than AB

sourceRecords.registryName: Alberta Energy Regulator — Directive 051: Injection and Disposal Wells
sourceRecords.supports: AER directive governing Class II disposal wells used to inject produced water (brine) or brine-equivalent fluids from upstream oil and gas operations in Alberta. Sets out wellbore design, integrity logging, operational monitoring, and reporting requirements for disposal wells.
limitations: Directive itself does not list per-operator volumes; per-well disposal volumes are reported separately under AER Directive 081 and via the AER's Class II Fluid Disposal application records
Per-operator records (including Onion Lake-specific disposal volumes) require an interactive search against AER application data, not retrievable via general web search

sourceRecords.registryName: CER — TMX Condition 113 Hydrostatic Testing Plan, Edmonton Terminal & Pump Stations (doc A7J3R7, 14 October 2020)
sourceRecords.supports: Hydrostatic Testing Plan filed by Trans Mountain Pipeline ULC pursuant to Condition 113 of CER Orders AO-002-XO-T260-008-2016 (Pump1), AO-002-XO-T260-009-2016 (Pump2), and AO-001-XO-T260-010-2016 (Tanks). Per the CER project record, the plan covers 15 pump stations grouped by region (Alberta pump stations, Northern British Columbia, Southern British Columbia) and details locations of water withdrawal and disposal sites, water withdrawal volume requirements, and flow rates.
limitations: Direct retrieval of the PDF via the docs2.cer-rec.gc.ca content server returned a fetch error on 2026-05-27; a reviewer should locate the document via CER project folder 2392873 (TMX) and the conditions index
Per-pump-station withdrawal volumes were not extractable from public summaries; verbatim figures require the PDF itself
Test medium is water without additives (or glycol/methanol in cold weather) — this is the testing input, not necessarily a Water Act-licensed source

projects.name: Capital Power Genesee Data Centre
projects.summary: Proposed up to 1,500 MW data-centre and power-generation complex co-located at the Capital Power Genesee site, AESO Phase II framework. Listed in plan §2 Tier 1.

projects.name: Mihta Askiy / Cree Ative Data Centre
projects.summary: Proposed up to 650 MW First-Nation-led data-centre and natural-gas generation project listed in the AESO Phase I queue. Listed in plan §2 Tier 1.

projects.name: Onion Lake Energy
projects.summary: First-Nation-owned upstream oil production on the AB/SK border, listed in plan §2 Tier 1. Authorizations may span Alberta, Saskatchewan, and IOGC federal registries. The `province` field above records the primary jurisdiction (AB) for project-level tagging only; the operation is cross-border and per-row `registryJurisdiction` values capture where each authorization actually sits. See per-row `crossBorderNote` on the SK-side row.

projects.name: Wapiti / Smoky sub-basin O&G aggregate
projects.summary: Aggregate placeholder for top oil-and-gas licensees in the Wapiti/Smoky sub-basin. Plan §2 Tier 1 notes basin allocation utilization at 99.64%; per-licensee identification is a Phase 1 verification task.

projects.name: TMX — Alberta pump-station water licences
projects.summary: Trans Mountain Expansion Alberta-side pump stations (Edmonton to BC border). Pipeline corridor is federal jurisdiction; Alberta pump-station water draws are provincially licensed.

authorizations.reviewerNote: Public sources confirm the data-centre proposal is named 'Polaris @ Genesee Energy Campus' (1.0-1.5 GW hyperscale, 2028 target in-service), anchored at the existing 1,800 MW Genesee Generating Station ~17 km north of Warburg, AB. The site has an existing cooling-pond and EPEA-approved water-use regime from the natural-gas expansion (IAAC ref 80059, 2014 'no EA required' decision). NOT YET POPULATED: (a) the specific Water Act licence number(s) governing site cooling water — must be retrieved interactively from the Alberta Authorizations Viewer; (b) allocated cooling-water volume (m³/yr); (c) the specific source water body (Lake Wabamun vs. North Saskatchewan River direct intake vs. cooling pond return-flow); (d) first-licensed date and licence conditions; (e) whether Polaris will draw from the existing site allocation or trigger a new Water Act approval. Stays at placeholder per Phase 2 plan §4: no volume number can be entered without a per-licence registry record.
Project is at AESO Phase I queue stage; water-licence application may not yet be public. Confirm whether the IAAC project page or a provincial approval has been published before populating volumes.

AB-side freshwater allocations for Onion Lake Energy upstream operations. Per Phase 2 plan §1 edge case (NRTA-002 decision #3): this row is a sibling to onion-lake-energy-sk-freshwater-placeholder under one cross-border operation, not an independent project. NOT YET POPULATED: (a) per-licence authorizationNumber from the Alberta Authorizations Viewer (general web search did not return Onion Lake-specific Water Act licence records on 2026-05-27); (b) allocatedVolume_m3_per_year; (c) sourceWatershed and sourceBody. Per project IngestionStatus.dataSovereigntyNote: limit Phase 2 ingestion to public registry fields only; defer Reserve-level / community-relevant data to Onion Lake Cree Nation governance per FNIGC OCAP principles. IOGC ledger access likely requires ATIP per Phase 2 plan §3 (deferred to Phase 4).

authorizations.crossBorderNote: Onion Lake Cree Nation Reserve straddles the AB/SK border; this row represents the SK-side water allocations for the same single operation captured in onion-lake-energy-freshwater-placeholder (AB-side). Verification must use the SK Water Security Agency, not the Alberta Authorizations Viewer. Per NRTA-002 decision #3, treat these AB and SK rows as siblings under one Indigenous-led economic operation rather than two independent projects. The project's primary `province` tag remains AB for project-level grouping; this row's `registryJurisdiction: SK` is the source-of-truth for verification routing.
authorizations.reviewerNote: SK-side freshwater allocations for Onion Lake Energy upstream operations. Phase 2 plan §3 flags the SK Water Security Agency registry as 'less publicly indexed' than AB — per-licence drill-down may require contacting the agency directly. NOT YET POPULATED: all licence-identity and volume fields. The SK Water Security Agency Act requires a Water Rights Licence for any non-domestic use; if Onion Lake Energy has SK operations, at least one such licence is expected to exist in the registry. Per project IngestionStatus.dataSovereigntyNote: limit Phase 2 ingestion to public registry fields only; defer Reserve-level / community-relevant data to Onion Lake Cree Nation governance per FNIGC OCAP principles.

AB-side produced-water disposal for Onion Lake Energy upstream operations. Disposal is regulated under AER Directive 051 (Class II injection/disposal wells); per-well disposal volumes are reported separately under AER Directive 081 and via the AER Class II Fluid Disposal application records. NOT YET POPULATED: (a) per-well authorization identifiers from the AER application records (interactive search required — not retrievable via general web search on 2026-05-27); (b) allocatedVolume_m3_per_year per disposal well or aggregated across the operation. A parallel SK-side row may be appropriate once SK-side produced-water disposal records have been confirmed (regulated under the Saskatchewan Ministry of Environment / SK energy regulator rather than the SK Water Security Agency); deferred until SK-side activity is mapped. Per project IngestionStatus.dataSovereigntyNote: limit Phase 2 ingestion to public registry fields only.
Identify the 1–2 top licensees in the Wapiti/Smoky sub-basin per plan §6. Per-licensee rows should replace this aggregate placeholder in Phase 2.

Public record confirms CER Condition 113 (issued under CER Orders AO-002-XO-T260-008-2016 / AO-002-XO-T260-009-2016 / AO-001-XO-T260-010-2016) requires Trans Mountain Pipeline ULC to file a Hydrostatic Testing Plan covering 15 pump stations grouped AB / Northern BC / Southern BC. The plan was filed 14 October 2020 as CER document A7J3R7 ('Condition 113 Hydrostatic Testing Plan Edm Terminal & Pumps Stations'). NOT YET POPULATED: (a) per-AB-pump-station hydrostatic test water volumes (m³) — should populate oneTimeVolume_m3 when retrieved, since hydrostatic test is one-time at construction (not annual); (b) source water bodies for each AB withdrawal site; (c) disposal destinations for spent test water; (d) site-specific mitigation and water-quality monitoring conditions. The A7J3R7 PDF was not directly retrievable via the docs2.cer-rec.gc.ca content server URL on 2026-05-27 — a reviewer should access via CER project folder 2392873 (TMX). authorizationNumber remains null because Condition 113 is a project-wide CER condition, not a per-station licence number; if AB-specific authorization identifiers exist in the filing, capture them then.

AB pump-station operational water draws (cooling and process) are provincially licensed even though the pipeline corridor is federal jurisdiction. The AB-side TMX route runs Edmonton/Sherwood Park → Spruce Grove → Yellowhead County → Hinton (per CER pipeline-profile records) and may correspond to multiple discrete Water Act licences along the corridor. NOT YET POPULATED: (a) per-pump-station Water Act licence numbers — must be retrieved interactively from the Alberta Authorizations Viewer (general web search did not surface specific TMX pump-station licence records on 2026-05-27); (b) allocated volumes per station; (c) source water bodies per station. Splitting this aggregate row into one row per pump station may be appropriate once the registry-side enumeration is complete; deferred until volume data is retrievable.

waterUseIndicators.name: Genesee DC total allocated water (m³/yr)
waterUseIndicators.description: Sum of allocated volumes across all cooling and process licences at the Genesee site once authorizations are populated.

waterUseIndicators.name: Mihta Askiy total allocated water (m³/yr)
waterUseIndicators.description: Sum of allocated volumes once the project's water-licence application is public.

waterUseIndicators.name: Onion Lake Energy total allocated water (m³/yr)
waterUseIndicators.description: Aggregate freshwater (AB + SK sibling rows) plus produced-water disposal across AB authorizations and IOGC federal records. Cross-border: rolls up sibling rows under one Indigenous-led operation that straddles the provincial line.

waterUseIndicators.name: Wapiti / Smoky O&G aggregate allocation (m³/yr)
waterUseIndicators.description: Aggregate placeholder until top licensees are identified and per-operator rows replace this row.

waterUseIndicators.name: TMX AB pump-station total allocated water (m³/yr)
waterUseIndicators.description: Aggregate of hydrostatic-test (one-time) and operational pump-station water across AB.

ingestionStatus.dataSovereigntyNote: First-Nation-led project; defer to Woodland Cree FN governance on what community-relevant data is appropriate to surface publicly. Limit Phase 1 ingestion to public registry fields only.

ingestionStatus.dataSovereigntyNote: Reserve-level water-use data may be subject to OCAP principles per plan §8.2. Ground IOGC ingestion in FNIGC guidance before pulling federal records. While this dataSovereigntyNote is set, validator rule P2-V14 blocks any Onion Lake authorization row from transitioning to `verified`; rows may move to `needs_verification` once volumes are populated from public sources.
